title: Bump github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient from 1.3.0 to 1.4.4
body:
Bumps [github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient) from 1.3.0 to 1.4.4.
Release notes *Sourced from [github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient's releases](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/releases).* > ## Last release with support for dep > v1.4.1 is the last release of go-dockerclient to support dep as a dependency management tool. dep may continue to work, but go-dockerclient no longer declares its dependencies using Gopkg.toml. > > ## Fix type for MemorySwappiness, PidsLimit and OOMKillDisable > This is a necessary breaking change to keep the client compatible with > the Docker remote API.
Commits - [`a439aa3`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/a439aa3a55481a7a3720fd63816220900003f1ab) Merge pull request [#795](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/issues/795) from grooverdan/pullImage_platform - [`684935a`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/684935acba0831c65e048d597fad049ab2f17420) ExportedImages to handle API 1.24/1.25 differences - [`e299729`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/e2997290e98b98d8af2458cdfd211a55f82e5d70) ExportImages to use streamUrl interface - [`c2ff0f0`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/c2ff0f067b672dcbd216f17cbbaeabcde050765f) Extend structs to have version tag, createImage as example - [`9daac7d`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/9daac7d4119c01df35ada3bea64c2c470df88ae5) integration_test: no arch for PullImage Platform - [`3200c66`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/3200c66e3bfba6a915d43ac80130b710f4622cae) PullImageOptions add Platform argument per API 1.32 - [`b257e87`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/b257e87c60bfa4e20178701d76c16fd7d38b97a2) Merge pull request [#798](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/issues/798) from fsouza/revert-797-skip-check-on-empty-versions-only - [`02310b1`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/02310b1f04006fcdd0747075c8e0e8fbfd638486) Revert "client: set SkipServerVersionCheck: true only if no version is specif... - [`8683b84`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/8683b8489904abef2a7a57a92fba76e42394dfc9) Merge pull request [#797](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/issues/797) from fsouza/skip-check-on-empty-versions-only - [`70e17b5`](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/commit/70e17b58439fd2e55bc99b138e8243db512c91f5) client: set SkipServerVersionCheck: true only if no version is specified - Additional commits viewable in [compare view](https://github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient/compare/v1.3.0...v1.4.4)

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://api.dependabot.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=1.3.0&new-version=1.4.4)](https://dependabot.com/compatibility-score.html?dependency-name=github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=1.3.0&new-version=1.4.4) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) --- **Note:** This repo was added to Dependabot recently, so you'll receive a maximum of 5 PRs for your first few update runs. Once an update run creates fewer than 5 PRs we'll remove that limit. You can always request more updates by clicking `Bump now` in your [Dependabot dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com).
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot use these labels` will set the current labels as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these reviewers` will set the current reviewers as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these assignees` will set the current assignees as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use this milestone` will set the current milestone as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot badge me` will comment on this PR with code to add a "Dependabot enabled" badge to your readme Additionally, you can set the following in your Dependabot [dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com): - Update frequency (including time of day and day of week) - Pull request limits (per update run and/or open at any time) - Out-of-range updates (receive only lockfile updates, if desired) - Security updates (receive only security updates, if desired) Finally, you can contact us by mentioning @dependabot.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
LGTM.  @nalind any concern bumping dockerclient?  I seem to remember some dependency in 1.3 but could be early Monday morning brain.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1885) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@vrothberg One problem with this tool, is it will constantly have conflicting files after the first one merges.  Is there a way to group them altogether, or is this likely just a first time issue, and they come in at a slow enough pace, that the conflict becomes much less likely to happen.
@rhatdan s/b a first time, though painful issue.  Once stuff rolls in going forward, it should be one at a time.

I also think it's a one-time issue. Now, we have a lot of dependencies to update at once. After that, I expect it calm down. I set the frequency to weekly checks. We can tweak that if we want to change.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1880) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> @vrothberg You must love playing whack-a-mole.

You may call me a dreamer :)

:pushpin: Commit 754700c has been approved by `rhatdan`

Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing c771c564fa9fd7b0bb2ca0e6d19ac649ab34eada to master...

OK, I won't notify you again about this release, but will get in touch when a new version is available. If you'd rather skip all updates until the next major or minor version, let me know by commenting `@dependabot ignore this major version` or `@dependabot ignore this minor version`.

If you change your mind, just re-open this PR and I'll resolve any conflicts on it.
